prompt: Run EVM analysis for ProjectAlpha. BAC is 480 FTE-hours, we're 45% complete, PV=240, EV=216, AC=250.
assertions: calculates all 11 metrics correctly: PV=240, EV=216, AC=250, CV=-34, SV=-24, CPI=0.864, SPI=0.9, EAC≈556, ETC≈306, VAC≈-76, TCPI≈1.15
CPI flagged as Red (<0.9)
SPI flagged as Amber (0.85-0.95)
TCPI assessed as challenging but achievable (<1.2)
includes narrative interpretation connecting numbers to actions
EAC formula selection is justified (not arbitrary)

prompt: This is our first EVM report for the project. We have no historical data.
assertions: acknowledges this is first report — no trend data available
presents single-point metrics with caveat about trend reliability
tags forecast as [SUPUESTO] until trend is established
recommends collecting ≥3 periods before trend analysis
does NOT fabricate historical data or show fake trend lines

prompt: Our agile team uses story points. How do we do EVM?
assertions: explains AgileEVM adaptation
uses story points as EV unit (not FTE-hours)
explains PV = planned story points per sprint
explains EV = completed (accepted) story points
mentions velocity-based forecasting as alternative to traditional EAC

prompt: Our TCPI is 1.35. What does this mean?
assertions: flags TCPI > 1.2 as statistically improbable recovery
recommends re-baselining, scope reduction, or additional funding
presents all three options with impact analysis
does NOT suggest 'the team just needs to work harder'
recommends escalation to steering committee
